data.title: FTC takes step toward reversing Chevron, Exxon board restrictions
data.description:
The U.S. Federal Trade Commission took a step toward potentially reversing bans on certain oil executives joining the boards of Chevron and Exxon Mobil that the Biden administration made a condition to letting them acquire two other oil producers.

Exxon, which acquired Pioneer Natural Resources, had agreed to bar former Pioneer CEO Scott Sheffield from its board, and Chevron, which acquired Hess , consented to a similar order keeping that company's CEO, John Hess, off its board.

The FTC said on Friday that it was seeking public comment on petitions filed by Sheffield, Chevron and Hess Corp seeking to reverse the bans.

Exxon, the No. 1 U.S. oil company, bought Pioneer in a deal worth $59.5 billion, and Chevron acquired Hess for $53 billion.

Both deals got the greenlight from the FTC, then led by Chair Lina Khan, on the condition that Hess and Sheffield be barred from the respective boards over concerns they would coordinate with members of the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries group of oil producers.

Both John Hess and Sheffield denied the allegations.

FTC Chairman Andrew Ferguson, then a commissioner, and fellow Republican Commissioner Melissa Holyoak voted against the agreements, saying they overstepped the agency's authority.

data.title: Abu Dhabi's ADNOC mulls bid for Aethon's US natgas assets, source says
data.description:
Abu Dhabi state oil company ADNOC is in the early stages of considering a bid for investment firm Aethon Energy Management's U.S. natural gas assets, according to a person familiar with the matter.

ADNOC has done a string of acquisitions in gas and chemicals, which along with LNG and renewables it considers as pillars for its future growth.

Last year, the energy giant bought a stake in NextDecade's liquefied natural gas export project in Texas along with a 20-year supply deal.

Deliberations regarding the U.S. energy-focused investment firm's assets also involve other parties, the source added.

Reuters in November reported that Aethon was exploring options for its natural gas production and midstream assets that included a sale or an initial public offering at a valuation of about $10 billion.

The upstream assets of Aethon, which primarily focus on the Haynesville shale formation in Louisiana and East Texas, constitute one of the largest privately held U.S. gas producers.

ADNOC and Aethon did not immediately respond to Reuters requests for comment.

Bloomberg News first reported that ADNOC was mulling a bid for Aethon's natural gas assets on Friday.

data.title: Uruguay Manufacturing Output at 4-month low
data.description:
Uruguay's manufacturing production rose by 4.8% year-on-year in February 2025, the biggest slowdown since September 2024, following a 7.1% acceleration in the previous month.

The year-on-year increase was due mainly to the expansion in oil refining, which saw a sharp 6388.4% rise as a result of ANCAP’s refinery maintenance in the first months of 2024, followed by metal production (113%) and metal products non-including machines and equipment (21.6%).

On the other hand, pharmaceutical products (-8.6%), rubber and plastic products (-7.7%) and food products (-1.6%) drove production down.

The index of hours worked (IHT) decreased by -3.7%, while the index of employed personnel (IPO) fell by -2.6%.

data.title: Mangione lawyers ask judge to prevent US prosecutors from seeking death penalty
data.description:
Luigi Mangione's lawyers on Friday asked a judge to prevent the U.S. government from seeking the death penalty in a case accusing him of shooting and killing the CEO of UnitedHealth Group's insurance division, in New York last year.

Mangione's lawyers said in a filing in New York federal court that U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi's April 1 announcement that prosecutors would seek the death penalty was politically motivated, breached government protocols for death penalty decisions and "indelibly prejudiced" the process.

"The United States government intends to kill Mr. Mangione as a political stunt," his lawyers said.

The Justice Department did not immediately respond to a request for comment.

data.title: Week's Best: Smart Selloff Moves
data.description:
By Amey Stone

The stock market's performance since President Donald Trump announced global tariffs last Wednesday has been unnerving, and uncertainty and market volatility are likely to continue, writes our contributing columnist, financial advisor Jonathan Shenkman. He urges investors to focus on the things that they can control. Rebalancing portfolios, selling losers, and buying on dips are all useful tactics during big selloffs.

In other most-read wealth management articles:

Safe spots for stashing cash. Stocks may be getting pummeled, but many cash-like investments continue to earn a respectable return. The average money-market fund boasts a 4.14% annualized seven-day yield, and firms like Vanguard and Schwab have money funds that offer more. Savings accounts vary from next to nothing to one that yields 4.54%. Other choices for holding cash include certificates of deposit, Treasury bonds, ultrashort bond funds, and multiyear guaranteed annuities.

Analyst shifts brokerage picks. Morgan Stanley analyst Michael Cyprys upgraded shares of Charles Schwab to Overweight and downgraded Robinhood Markets' stock to Equal Weight in a sign of how tariff-related market turmoil is changing expectations for brokerage and wealth management companies. Current volatility and uncertainty may spur retail investors to disengage from markets for a prolonged period, he writes.

Advisors explain market spike. Stocks soared Wednesday as President Donald Trump paused some of his tariffs. The historic stock rally provided advisors an opportunity to point out to clients why bailing out of the market after selloffs isn't a smart strategy. In our latest Barron's Advisor Big Q, we asked advisors to share what they are telling clients. They told us they are reminding clients of the importance of sound financial planning and avoiding rash decisions.

Money fund assets slip? Even as investors fled stocks, assets in money-market funds fell slightly over the past week. The likely reason is investors using cash held in money funds to pay taxes. Total money-market fund assets decreased by $25 billion from the previous week but remain near a record high, according to the Investment Company Institute.

Vanguard's economic outlook sours. Vanguard is cutting its forecast for U.S. economic growth and raising its expectations for inflation to account for the potential impact of President Donald Trump's tariffs. Current trends could mean the economy is headed for stagflation, says Joe Davis, Vanguard's chief economist. "We are dancing with recession," he writes.

Write to Amey Stone at amey.stone@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: APA Director Bought the Dip in the Energy Producer's Stock
data.description:
By Ed Lin

APA stock has tumbled to multiyear lows, and one director at the energy producer bought a large block of shares.

Shares of the holding company of oil and natural-gas explorer Apache were already down 8% for the year as of April 2, the date on which President Donald Trump chose to make his "Liberation Day" announcement of a raft of global tariffs after the market close.

Energy investors didn't like what they heard, and APA stock dove 16%, to $17.74, on April 3, the next trading day. APA director Chansoo Joung bought the dip that day, paying $1.37 million for 75,000 shares, an average price of $18.25. He now owns 140,285 APA shares in a personal account, according to a form that Joung filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

APA didn't respond to a request to make Joung available for comment on his stock purchase. An APA director since February 2011, Joung spent most of his career in finance working with energy companies, with stints at Warburg Pincus and Goldman Sachs Group.

He last purchased APA stock on the open market about three years ago, when the stock was more expensive. Joung paid $1.32 million on Feb. 25, 2022, for 40,000 shares, an average price of $32.92 each.

APA stock has continued to sink in the days after the tariffs announcement, trading at around $14, a level that APA stock last traded at in February 2021.

Write to Ed Lin at edward.lin@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Market Chatter: Jefferies Financial to Raise $600 Million for Gulf Coast Ammonia
data.description:
Jefferies Financial Group is looking to raise roughly $600 million in the US high-yield bond market for Gulf Coast Ammonia, Bloomberg reported Friday, citing people close to the matter.

The proceeds will be used to refinance the specialty chemicals maker's existing debt of approximately $574 million, which was raised in January 2020 for the development of its Texas-based anhydrous ammonia plant, the report said.

A deal could be made in the coming two weeks, the report quoted the people as saying.

Representatives of Jefferies and Gulf Coast Ammonia didn't immediately respond to a request for comment from MT Newswires.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: FTC Seeks Public Comment on Petitions for Exxon, Chevron Megadeals
data.description:
By Connor Hart

The Federal Trade Commission is seeking comments from the public regarding two separate petitions involving multi-billion dollar oil deals: Exxon Mobil's purchase of Pioneer Natural Resources and Chevron's acquisition of Hess.

Scott Sheffield, the founder and former chief executive of Pioneer, last month filed a petition to the FTC, requesting the agency set aside a final consent order regarding the companies' $60 billion merger.

The order, which the FTC approved in January, prohibited Exxon from nominating Sheffield to its board of directors. It additionally barred him from serving in an advisory role to Exxon's board or management team.

Sheffield in the petition alleged the order was unsupported by any antitrust law, and violates his constitutional and other legally-protected rights.

Also in March, Chevron and Hess filed a petition asking the FTC to set aside its consent order regarding their $53 billion deal, which was issued in January and prohibits Chevron from nominating Hess CEO John Hess to Chevron's board.

The FTC said Friday that the public will have 30 days, or until May 12, to submit comments regarding both petitions. After the comment period closes, the agency will vote to determine how to resolve both petitions.

Write to Connor Hart at connor.hart@wsj.com

data.title: Walmart Gets Buy Signal from Mizuho as Safe Bet in Unsteady Market
data.description:
Mizuho analyst David Bellinger says Walmart is a smart pick for investors trying to stay steady in a rocky market. He's sticking with an Outperform rating and sees the stock heading toward 105 dollars, which would be about 15 percent above where it trades now.

According to Bellinger, Walmart gives investors several ways to win during uncertain times. It continues to pull in price-conscious shoppers and has made solid progress in e-commerce, helping the company stay strong even when the economy slows.

Walmart has also become the country's largest grocery chain, which makes it a go-to source for everyday essentials. That role could give it an edge if the U.S. slips into a recession, Bellinger added.

Even though about one third of Walmart's products are imported, mostly from China and Mexico, Mizuho believes the company is taking smart steps to keep prices in check including negotiating with suppliers when needed. Bellinger also floated a more bullish scenario where Walmart stock climbs to 125 dollars this year. So far in 2025, the stock is up about 2 percent.

data.title: Bristol-Myers Squibb Investors Likely to Focus on Performance of New Schizophrenia Therapy, UBS Says
data.description:
Bristol-Myers Squibb investors will be focused on the trajectory of Cobenfy, its schizophrenia therapy, on its first full quarter on the market, UBS said in a note emailed Friday.

The brokerage believes the company will have some insight into the drug's indication expansion, based on the phase 3 study of adjunctive schizophrenia. The results from the trial are expected in Q2.

On the earnings front, UBS said it was estimating Q1 EPS of $1.52, in line with expectations.

In its note, UBS highlighted a number of headwinds heading into the earnings, such as about $500 million in destocking, Medicare Part D redesign, and erosion of the company's legacy portfolio. But, these headwinds were already baked into consensus expectations.

Consensus for Eliquis and Revlimid sales could be too low, UBS said, as data from IQVIA indicated that prescriptions for both medicines was strong. It was forecasting Q1 Eliquis sales of $3.40 billion, ahead of consensus' $3.34 billion, and Revlimid sales at $838 million, also above the consensus' $802 million.

All in, UBS said its total sales estimate for the quarter was 0.8% above consensus.

The firm slashed its price target for Bristol-Myers to $54 from $60, with a neutral rating.

data.title: Tariff Turmoil Has Split the Bond Market in Two
data.description:
By Patti Domm

Tariffs have given the Treasury market a split personality. That is likely to stay the case until the extent of the economic hit is clear.

Bonds are traditionally a haven, or ballast against swings in risk assets like stocks. that role was challenged this week by an unusually volatile Treasury market. But the volatility has affected longer-duration debt more than shorter maturities.

The yield on the benchmark 10-year Treasury note fell to a low 3.85% before Monday's U.S. trading began but rocketed above 4.5% Friday. The two-year moved from 3.8 to just under 4%. At the same time, the dollar has been falling sharply against global currencies.

"It just seems like a capital flight right now," said Brian Rehling, head of global fixed-income strategy at the Wells Fargo Investment Institute. "It's kind of a sell America trade."

Bond strategists say the short-end of the Treasury curve — durations of two years and lower — should remain more stable because the safe-haven effect is stronger there. The two-year yield is also anchored more by the federal reserve's interest rate targets than the long end. Yields move opposite price.

The 10-year yield, meanwhile, is vulnerable to factors other than inflation, growth, and other economic fundamentals. It reacted this week with an unusual skittishness, driven in part by hedge funds unwinding leverage trades that play an arbitrage between futures and actual bond prices. There was also a clear exodus of investors leaving the U.S. assets that added to Treasury volatility.

"There are two bond markets. There's the front end, and now the other end," said George Goncalves, head of U.S. macro strategy at MUFG. The Treasury market has become segmented as a result of the forces unleashed by tariffs, he said. Similar effects happened during Covid and the financial crisis.

Goncalves said he expects the 10-year yield to trade in a volatile range between roughly 3.75% to 4.5%. It was challenging the top of that range Friday. "You could break out of these levels if you really start to come under liquidity pressures," he said.

The two-year, on the other hand, has been less volatile, and should remain so, Goncalves said. With the fed unlikely to raise rates, "it isn't going much further," he said

The 10-year yield influences the borrowing rates for home mortgages and a whole range of consumer and business loans. It also is closely watched by stock investors, and its unnerving move higher in yields has happened as stocks sold off sharply.

President Donald Trump's April 2 tariff announcement set off the volatility. The tariffs were far higher than many in the markets anticipated. His April 9 pause of tariffs for 90 days sent the stock market into a huge one-day rally, but the concerns about the impact of tariffs on the global economy remain.

The market has also been watching congressional efforts to move forward with a budget, said Rehling. Expectations of higher deficit spending could also put upward pressure on yields.

But tariffs are the main concern for the market. The rise in volatility alongside lower stock prices has forced some hedge funds to exit their positions.

"That is really what hammered the market over the last couple of days," Rehling said. "You had this funding crunch, huge, forced liquidations for various reasons," he said.

Investors looking to diversify away from the U.S. would be selling Treasuries, sending yields up and the dollar down.

Many tariffs remain after Trump's pause, especially on China. Trump set those at 145%, and China retaliated with 125% tariffs on U.S. goods. The U.S. exported more than $400 billion in goods to the U.S. last year, and imported about $150 billion.

As the dollar fell this week, rumors swirled that China is selling U.S. Treasuries.

There isn't evidence of any such sales, said Marc Chandler, chief market strategist at Bannockburn Global Forex. Foreign central banks and other government entities increased their purchases of Treasuries this week, according to Federal Reserve data.

"Why would China sell Treasuries? That would hurt them," Chandler said. China has a better economic weapon in its control of the processing of many rare earth minerals, used in semiconductors, electric vehicles, and other critical technology.

The dollar index briefly fell through the psychological 100 level Friday in intraday trading. It remains volatile. The index has fallen for two straight weeks.

"I don't think we've bottomed by any means," said Chandler.

Write to editors@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: GM to halt EV van production in Ontario to adjust for market demand
data.description:
GM is temporarily halting production of its electric commercial van at its assembly plant in Ontario due to slow sales, the company and union representing workers there said.

The Detroit automaker is temporarily laying off 1,200 workers at the plant as a result, according to Unifor, the union representing the workers. The shutdown is not related to recently imposed auto tariffs, the company said.

GM said it's making "operational and employment adjustments to balance inventory and align production schedules with current demand," at the CAMI Assembly plant where it makes Chevrolet BrightDrop electric vans.

The layoffs will start on April 14, according to the union, with workers returning in May for limited production. Production will temporarily cease until October 2025. During the downtime, GM said it plans to retool the plant to prepare for production of the 2026 model year BrightDrop vans.

When production resumes in October, the plant will operate on a single shift, which will result in the indefinite layoff of nearly 500 workers, the union said.

The automaker said in a statement it "remains committed to the future of BrightDrop and the CAMI plant and will support employees through the transition. This adjustment is directly related to responding to market demand and re-balancing inventory. Production of BrightDrop and EV battery assembly will remain at CAMI."

Unifor National President Lana Payne in a statement called the move "a crushing blow to hundreds of working families in Ingersoll and the surrounding region who depend on this plant."

"General Motors must do everything in its power to mitigate job loss during this downturn, and all levels of government must step up to support Canadian auto workers and Canadian-made products,” Payne said.

GM reported sales of 274 BrightDrop vans in the first quarter, up from the 256 reported in the first quarter of 2024.

Pierre Poilievre, a Conservative Party leader in Canada who is running for prime minister, said in a post on X that he's committed to protecting Canadian auto workers.

"We will keep them working, so they can keep their jobs as we fight through this mess," Poilievre wrote. "A Conservative Government will push hard to put an end to these tariffs and get a quick but fair deal that protects our sovereignty and our economy."

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Friday at 3 PM ET
data.description:
China Raises Tariffs on U.S. to 125%, Says More Tit-for-Tat Would Be a Joke

Beijing expressed weariness with the repeated rounds of increases that threaten to make much of the trade between the world's two biggest economies grind to a halt.

Fed's Williams Sees Greater Inflation, Slower Growth After Tariffs

Current monetary policy stance "is in the right place " to manage those risks, says the New York Fed president.

'From Anxious to Petrified': Consumer Sentiment Plunges Further

The closely watched University of Michigan index hit its second-lowest reading on record, dragged down by fears of higher prices and unemployment.

Producer Prices Fell in March as Energy, Food Costs Declined

Wholesale prices dropped last month, with substantial declines in goods prices driven by lower food and energy costs.

U.S. Stocks Rise Despite Consumer Sentiment Plunge, China Tariff Retaliation

The Dow industrials, the S&P 500 and the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite moved higher in midday trading.

Small Sellers of Fireworks, Ski Apparel and Other Imports Can't Escape Tariff War

Uncertainty about President Trump's tariffs is leading many small businesses to interrupt or halt production because they can't absorb price hikes and lack leverage to negotiate.

Trump Has a $350 Billion Deal for Europe: Buy Our Energy

The continent has sharply increased its U.S. purchases in recent years, with little appetite to buy more.

Europe's Governments Pledge Unity in Tariff Fight

The eurozone will stand together in support of retaliation against the U.S. if talks to resolve the trade conflict are unsuccessful, Ireland's finance minister said.

Dollar Slides to Multiyear Lows as U.S.-China Tariff War Intensifies

The greenback fell sharply in early European trading, hitting a three-year low against a basket of major currencies and extending overnight losses.

Week Ahead for FX, Bonds: U.S. Retail Sales and ECB, BOC Decisions in Focus as Market Digests Tariff Turmoil

After a week of huge volatility-with hefty selling in U.S. Treasurys and the U.S. dollar due to President Trump's sweeping trade tariffs and a subsequent 90-day reprieve for many of them-investors will be hoping for a quieter week ahead.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Friday at 3 PM ET
data.description:
General Motors to Suspend Production, Lay Off 500 at Electric Vehicle Plant in Ontario

General Motors has temporarily halted production of its BrightDrop electric delivery van as the automaker will initiate temporary layoffs starting April 14 and workers will return in May for limited production.

Wall Street Sounds an Alarm: The Economy Is at Risk Thanks to Tariffs

JPMorgan Chase's Jamie Dimon and BlackRock's Larry Fink both said the turbulence wasn't like prior financial crises.

Ireland's Privacy Watchdog Probes Musk's Grok AI Model

Ireland's data privacy watchdog said it is looking into whether Grok has been illegally trained on European user posts on Musk's X social media platform.

Morgan Stanley's Profit Surged on Trading Revenue

The bank's profit increased to $4.32 billion in the first three months of the year, or $2.60 per share, surpassing the $2.18 per share analysts expected.

Wells Fargo Profit Rises, Boosted by Lower Expenses

Wells Fargo booked a bigger profit in the first quarter, as lower expenses helped offset a decline in net interest income.

BlackRock's Assets Hit Record as Net Income Falls

BlackRock's quarterly net income fell slightly from a year earlier, while the investment firm's assets under management edged higher to a record $11.6 trillion.

Tesla Deletes Buy Option for Model S and X From China Website

Tesla has removed the option for purchases of new Model S and Model X vehicles on its China website as trade tensions between the world's two largest economies escalate.

BP Expects First-Quarter Upstream Production to Fall

The company anticipates lower upstream production than the previous quarter due to already announced divestments in Egypt and Trinidad.

Novo Remade Denmark. The Danes Are Still Adjusting.

Denmark's economy was reshaped by Novo Nordisk and obesity drugs. That identity now is under attack on multiple fronts, from science to geopolitics.

Twinkies' New Owner Courts a Novel Group of Snackers: Stoners

J.M. Smucker is hoping to boost sales of Hostess snacks by connecting them to modern cultural moments, like 4/20 cannabis celebrations.

data.title: Fastenal First-Quarter Revenue Tops Views; Shares Rise
data.description:
Fastenal on Friday logged better-than-expected first-quarter revenue amid an increase in unit sales, sending the company's shares higher.

Net sales of the distributor of industrial and construction supplies rose 3.4% year over year to $1.96 billion for the quarter ended March 31, topping the FactSet-polled consensus of $1.95 billion. Net income was unchanged at $0.52 a share, in line with Wall Street's estimates.

Shares of Fastenal were up 6.9% in Friday late-afternoon trade. The stock has risen 13% so far this year.

The company attributed higher unit sales mainly to growth in the number of customer sites spending at least $10,000 per month with Fastenal. Price levels continued to be "relatively stable" in the first quarter, the company said in a statement.

Daily sales increased 5%, marking the company's strongest growth rate since the second quarter of 2023, Chief Financial Officer Holden Lewis said on an earnings conference call, according to a FactSet transcript.

However, "feedback from regional leadership continues to reflect sluggish end market demand, despite generally favorable outlooks," Lewis said. "Customer tone did seem to shift from the steady improvement we've seen since the (US presidential) election to plateauing as trade policy created some caution."

China on Friday reportedly raised its tariffs on US imports to 125%, in response to US President Donald Trump's move to hike duties on Beijing to 145%. Earlier in the week, Trump announced a 90-day pause for certain tariffs for non-retaliating countries.

Fastenal has taken certain actions that are likely to contribute 3% to 4% of price in the second quarter, with the potential for that to double in the second half the year, Lewis told analysts. "We have taken no actions on the deferred portion of (reciprocal tariffs) but may need to, should those ultimately go into force."

The daily sales rate of fasteners improved to 1.1% in the quarter from a 4.4% drop a year earlier, while the rate of non-fasteners accelerated to 6.8% from 5.2%. By end markets, manufacturing logged daily sales growth of 6.8%, advancing from a 2.6% increase a year earlier, while non-manufacturing dropped 0.6% versus a flat reading.

data.title: Argentina monthly inflation shoots past expectations in March
data.description:
Argentina's monthly inflation rate again sped up in March, data released on Friday showed, a nasty shock to libertarian President Javier Milei's strict austerity measures.

The monthly rate came in at 3.7%, above Reuters' forecast of 2.6% and higher than February's 2.4%.

Inflation in the 12 months through March hit 55.9%, slowing from the previous month's 66.9% rate, but still above the 54.2% rate expected from analysts polled by Reuters.

While inflation has fallen sharply under Milei's administration from triple-digit annual rates, monthly price increases have stalled in recent months between 2% and 3%. March's rate, however, puts the government even further from its goal of bringing monthly inflation closer to 1%.

The education sector logged the highest increase, of 22%, on the end of the Southern Hemisphere summer and the return to classes.

Milei's administration has rolled out tough austerity measures which have helped bring inflation down from the once-painful triple-digit increases annually. However, the spending pinch has also fueled protests, with workers on Thursday paralyzing the country in a nationwide strike.

The government is now looking to ease capital controls, a measure which could come after a $20 billion deal with the International Monetary Fund (IMF) expected to be announced later on Friday.

data.title: Xcyte Digital Announces Delay in Filling Fiscal 2024 and Q1 Fiscal 2025 Financial Results and Cease Trade Order
data.description:
POMPANO BEACH, FLORIDA / ACCESS Newswire / April 11, 2025 / Xcyte Digital Corp. ("Xcyte", or the "Company"), a trusted global events technology company, specializing in next-generation event solutions, discloses today that the filing of its annual financial statements for the fiscal year ended September 30, 2024, the related management's discussion and analysis and the related officer certifications of the annual filings and for Q1 Fiscal 2025 (collectively, the "2024 Annual Financial Filings" and "Q1 2025 Financial Filings") were not completed by the deadline of January 28, 2025, and March 3, 2025 respectively (the "Filing Deadlines"). As a result, before markets opened on April 9, 2025, the Canadian Investment Regulatory Organization issued a cease trade order (the "Cease Trade Order") against the Corporation and its securities have been halted from trading on the TSX Venture Exchange.

The Company agrees with the Canadian Investment Regulatory Organization with this Cease Trade Order as current public information does not adequately reflect the Company's performance as a result of its recent acquisitions.

The Company is working diligently and expeditiously with MNP in order to complete the 2024 Annual Financial Filings and Q1 2025 Financial Filings as soon as practicable, and integrate disparate internal systems to ensure future filing deadlines are met. The Company currently anticipates that it will be in a position to remedy any filing defaults and return to compliance by completing the 2024 Annual Financial Filings and Q1 2025 Financial Filingson or before April 30, 2025.

About Xcyte Digital Corp.

Xcyte Digital Corp. is a trusted global events technology partner, specializing in next generation event technology for physical, hybrid, virtual, immersive and phone-based events. Combining proprietary technology with a robust partner ecosystem, Xcyte offers both do-it-yourself and managed services, ensuring secure and scalable solutions worldwide. Thousands of clients, from innovative startups to major corporations, rely on Xcyte's cost-effective, multi-platform subscription model to meet their event needs. Xcyte Digital is headquartered in Canada and the USA, with operations across the globe. Visit us at xcytedigital.com.

For more information contact:

Xcyte Digital Corp.

Randy Selman, Chief Executive Officer and Director

Phone: (647) 777 7501

Email: investment@xcytedigital.com

Investor Relations

Nikhil Thadani, Sophic Capital

Email: nik@sophiccapital.com

Phone: (647) 777 7501

Neither TSX Venture Exchange nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in the policies of the TSX Venture Exchange) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

Cautionary Note Regarding Forward-Looking Statements

Certain information contained in this release constitute forward-looking statements or information under Canadian securities legislation. Generally, forward-looking statements can be identified by the use of forward-looking terminology such as "will", "expects", "anticipates" or variations of such words and phrases or statements that certain actions, events or results "will" occur. In particular, ‎forward-looking ‎statements in this release include statements regarding the anticipated timing for the filing of the Annual and Q1 Filings. Forward-looking statements are based on the opinions and estimates of management as of the date such statements are made and are subject to known and unknown risks, uncertainties and other factors that may cause actual results to be materially different from those expressed or implied by such forward-looking statements, including that the Annual and Q1 Filings may not be completed in the time anticipated. Although management of the Company has attempted to identify important factors that could cause actual results to differ materially from those contained in the forward-looking statements, there may be other factors that cause results not to be as anticipated, estimated or intended. There can be no assurance that such statements will prove to be accurate, as actual results and future events could differ materially from those anticipated in such statements. The Company cautions readers of this news release not to place undue reliance on the forward-looking statements contained in this release as many factors could cause actual results or conditions to differ materially from current expectations. Additional information on these and other risk factors that could affect the Company's operations are outlined in the Company's continuous disclosure documents that can be found on SEDAR+ (www.sedarplus.ca) under Xcyte's issuer profile. Xcyte does not intend and disclaims any obligation, except as required by law, to update or revise any forward-looking statements, whether because of new information, future events, or otherwise.

SOURCE: Xcyte Digital

data.title: Couchbase, Staar Surgical, Zymeworks, and More Stocks See Action From Activist Investors
data.description:
These disclosures are from 13Ds filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission. 13Ds are filed within 10 days of an entity's attaining more than 5% in any class of a company's securities. Subsequent changes in holdings or intentions must be reported in amended filings. This material is from April 3 through April 9, 2025. Source: VerityData

Increases in Holdings

Couchbase(BASE) Haveli Investments increased its stake in the cloud database company to 5,195,601 shares. Haveli Investments did so through the purchase of 990,433 Couchbase shares from March 28 through April 3, 2025, at per share prices of $14.60 to $15.96. Following the latest purchases, Haveli owns 9.8% of Couchbase's outstanding stock. Shares of Couchbase have gained roughly 3.2% since the start of 2025.

Staar Surgical(STAA) Broadwood Partners increased its stake in the manufacturer of eyeglass lenses to 13,358,164 shares. Broadwood Partners did so through the purchase of 1,379,066 Staar Surgical shares from March 4 through April 7, 2025, at per share prices of $14.97 to $17.99. Following the latest purchases, Broadwood Partners owns 27.1% of Staar Surgical's outstanding stock. Shares of Staar Surgical have lost over a third of their value since the start of 2025.

Aeva Technologies(AEVA) Sylebra Capital raised its stake in the autonomous sensing technology company to 16,228,553 shares. Sylebra Capital did so through the purchase of 5,849,249 Aeva shares from March 24 through March 31, 2025, at per share prices of $4.36 to $6.77. Following the latest purchases, Sylebra Capital continues to own 29.6% of Aeva's outstanding stock. Shares of Aeva have gained approximately 40.1% in value since the beginning of this year.

Mid Penn Bancorp(MPB) General American Capital raised its stake in the Harrisburg, Pa.--based regional bank to 1,410,644 shares. General American did so through the purchase of 220,565 Mid Penn Bancorp shares from Jan. 7 through April 1, 2025, at per share prices of $25.62 to $30.25. Following the latest purchases, General American owns 7.3% of Mid Penn Bancorp's outstanding stock. Shares of Mid Penn Bancorp have lost about 10% in value since the start of 2025.

Zymeworks(ZYME) EcoR1 Capital raised its stake in the oncology-focused biopharmaceutical company to 17,699,774 shares. EcoR1 Capital purchased 841,356 Zymeworks shares from March 18 through April 4, 2025, at per share prices of $11.17 to $13.08. Following the latest purchases, EcoR1 Capital continues to own 25.4% of Zymeworks' outstanding stock. Shares of Zymeworks have lost a third of their value since the beginning of this year.

Decreases in Holdings

Runway Growth Finance(RWAY) OCM Growth Holdings lowered its stake in the specialty finance company to 9,779,668 shares. OCM Growth did so through the sale of 1,000,000 Runway Growth shares pursuant to a Rule 144 sale on April 1, 2025, at an average price of $10.35 per share. Following the latest sale, OCM Growth Holdings continues to own approximately 26.2% of Runway Growth Finance's outstanding stock. Shares of Runway Growth Finance have lost roughly 18.4% of their value since the beginning of this year.

Email: editors@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: General Motors to Suspend Production, Lay Off 500 at Electric Vehicle Plant in OntarioUpdate
data.description:
By Robb M. Stewart and Paul Vieira

General Motors has temporary halted production of its BrightDrop electric delivery van and plans to cut workers at its Ontario plant when it resumes scaled-back output, labor union Unifor said Friday.

The Detroit automaker confirmed it is making operational and employment adjustments to "balance inventory and align production schedules with current demand." The company said it was committed to the future of BrightDrop and its CAMI plant in Ingersoll, Ontario, where production of the van and EV battery assembly would remain.

The decision from GM adds to worries in Canada about the wider ramifications from President Trump's trade policy on its manufacturing sector and economy. The Trump administration last week imposed a 25% tariff on foreign-made cars, in a bid to persuade auto makers to move operations from countries such as Canada to the U.S.

Prime Minister Mark Carney said earlier in the day the tariff-fueled market turmoil of the past week has led to a "marked tightening" in financial conditions, and is weighing on Canada's labor market. The economy shed about 33,000 jobs in March, the worst one-month performance in three years.

Last week, Stellantis NV said it would temporarily halt production at its minivan plant in Windsor, Ontario, for two weeks, a day after the White House's tariff aimed at imported vehicles went into effect.

GM's decision was due to weak demand for the EV van, and not related to the U.S. administration's trade policy, according to a person briefed on the matter. Recent data from GM indicated the company sold 274 BrightDrop vans in the first quarter, a 7% increase from the same period a year-ago. Overall, sales of GM vehicles rose 16.7% in the first quarter.

Lana Payne, the president of Unifor, Canada's largest private-sector union, said tariffs imposed on the country and Trump's policies regarding EVs played a role in GM's decision. The administration's policies are "disrupting investment and freezing future order projections," Payne said.

Unifor said GM will initiate temporary layoffs starting April 14, and workers will return in May for limited production. It said the company informed it production would then be halted until October, during which time GM plans retooling work to prepare for the 2026 model year of commercial EVs.

When production does resume, the plant will operate a single shift for the foreseeable future, which is expected to result in the indefinite layoff of almost 500 workers, Unifor said. The union represents more than 1,200 workers at CAMI, where BrightDrop production began in early 2023

Payne called on the company to do everything in its power to mitigate job losses, and for all levels of government to step up and support Canadian auto workers and Canadian-made products.

Write to Robb M. Stewart at robb.stewart@wsj.com

data.title: AIBOTICS, INC. SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
AIBotics, Inc., a company dedicated to the study and development of psychedelics for mental health treatment and the integration of AI in robotics, has released its 2024 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the evolving market landscape.

Financial Highlights

  • Sales: $0 million. The company did not have any revenue from operations for the years ended December 31, 2024, and 2023.
  • Net Loss: $1.85 million. The net loss increased from $1.18 million in 2023 to $1.85 million in 2024, primarily due to increased general and administrative expenses.
  • Net Loss Per Share: $0.14. The net loss per share increased from $0.08 in 2023 to $0.14 in 2024, reflecting the higher net loss.
  • General and Administrative Expenses: $1.63 million. Increased by 165% from $0.62 million in 2023, driven by higher amortization expenses, board compensation, product development expenses, and consulting fees.
  • Interest Expense: $0.21 million. Decreased by 62% from $0.56 million in 2023, primarily due to less debt discount amortization, partially offset by additional interest expense from default interest on convertible note payables.

Business Highlights

  • Business Overview: AIBotics, Inc. focuses on the study and development of psychedelics for mental health treatment, leveraging technology from its parent company, Ehave, Inc., to collect research and clinical data. The company aims to use AI to create disruptive products that enhance patient care and streamline medical processes.
  • New Product Launches: AIBotics introduced the Phill Robot, an AI-powered massage robot, and the Milkyway, a smart refrigerator for breast milk storage. The Phill Robot offers a personalized massage experience with advanced AI algorithms, while the Milkyway provides efficient breast milk storage with a vertical rotating system.
  • Business Strategy and Market: The company is positioning itself in the growing psychedelic drugs market, which is projected to grow 12.4% annually, reaching $10.75 billion by 2027. AIBotics aims to be a leader in supplying naturally-derived psilocybin compounds and forming partnerships with labs and research institutions.
  • Government Regulation: AIBotics supports research and development of therapies using psilocybin in compliance with the US Controlled Substances Act and FDA regulations. The company is not directly impacted by the CSA as it does not engage in the manufacturing or sale of psilocybin.
  • Future Outlook: AIBotics plans to continue leveraging its relationship with Ehave, Inc. to integrate digital and data therapeutics in mental health treatments. The company is also focusing on expanding its AI and robotics applications to improve quality of life.
  • Segment Information: The company operates as one segment, focusing on AI and robotics to drive innovation across multiple industries, including healthcare and manufacturing.
  • New Production Launches: AIBotics acquired assets from Philon Labs, including intellectual property related to the Phill Robot and Milkyway products, marking a shift towards AI in robotics.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Strategic Initiatives: The company is focusing on integrating AIBotics, Inc., which was acquired subsequent to December 31, 2024, as part of its strategic initiative to enhance its business operations. Additionally, the company is actively pursuing additional equity financing through discussions with investment bankers and private investors to improve liquidity and support its growth-oriented transition.
  • Capital Management: The company issued 200,000 shares of Series B Preferred Stock to settle consideration for intangible assets acquired from Philon Labs, LLC. It also increased its authorized shares of common stock from 100,000,000 to 467,000,000. The company has a working capital deficiency of $4,666,666 as of December 31, 2024, and plans to fund operations with cash on hand and additional financing. The company did not pay any dividends and intends to retain earnings to finance business development.
  • Future Outlook: The company plans to continue funding its operations through the issuance of debt or equity and is focused on developing profitable operations. Management is committed to raising capital and developing its business, with no assurance of success. The company is also evaluating the impact of new accounting standards on its financial statements and disclosures.

Challenges and Risks

  • Regulatory Challenges: The company faces challenges in aligning with the evolving legal and policy landscape of mental health regulation, particularly concerning the use of psychedelics. The regulatory environment is complex, with significant oversight from the FDA and DEA, which could impact the company's ability to conduct research and develop products.
  • Operational Challenges: The integration of AI technology in healthcare and wellness products, such as the Phill Robot and Milkyway, presents operational challenges in terms of product development and market acceptance.
  • Financial Challenges: The company reported no revenue for 2024 and 2023, highlighting financial challenges. The increase in general and administrative expenses, primarily due to amortization, board compensation, and product development, indicates operational cost pressures. The company has a significant working capital deficiency and an accumulated deficit, which poses a risk to its financial stability. Management plans to fund operations through cash on hand and additional financing, but the ability to secure such financing remains uncertain.
  • Market Risks: Potential market risks include fluctuations in the regulatory environment for psychedelics and the acceptance of AI-driven wellness products. The company's financial condition is also vulnerable to changes in interest rates and the availability of capital.

SEC Filing: AIBOTICS, INC. [ TPIA ] - 10-K - Apr. 11, 2025

data.title: Market Chatter: Alphabet's Google Lays Off Hundreds of Employees From Platform, Devices Unit
data.description:
Alphabet's Google unit hundreds of employees from the platforms and devices division, which handles Android, Pixel phones and the Chrome browser, The Information reported Thursday, citing a person with knowledge of the matter.

"Since combining the platforms and devices teams last year, we've focused on becoming more nimble and operating more effectively and this included making some job reductions in addition to the voluntary exit program that we offered in January," a Google spokesperson said in a statement, according to The Information.

Google didn't immediately respond to a request for comment from MT Newswires.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.
